info.description: Intelligent Docker Container Update Manager with Auto-Restart
info.title: TideWatch

get.description: Return aggregated analytics for the dashboard.

post.description:
Cancel setup and disable authentication (only during initial setup).

This endpoint allows users to cancel the setup process from the setup page.
It can only be called before setup is complete to prevent unauthorized
auth mode changes.
Authenticate admin user and set JWT token in httpOnly cookie.
Logout admin user by clearing JWT cookie.

get.description: Get current admin user profile.

put.description: Update admin profile (email and full name only).

get.description:
Handle OIDC callback from provider (public).

Query Parameters:
    code: Authorization code from provider
    state: State parameter for CSRF protection

Returns:
    Redirect to frontend with JWT cookie set

Get OIDC configuration (admin only).

Returns configuration with client_secret masked for security.

put.description:
Update OIDC configuration (admin only).

Args:
    oidc_config: New OIDC configuration

Returns:
    Success message

post.description:
Link OIDC account to admin with password verification (public).

This endpoint is called after OIDC login when the admin account requires
password verification to link the OIDC identity.

Security:
- Max 3 password attempts per token
- Token expires after 5 minutes
- One-time use (token deleted after success)

Args:
    token: Pending link token from callback redirect
    password: Admin password for verification
    request: FastAPI request for header extraction
    response: FastAPI response for cookie setting
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Redirect to frontend with JWT cookie set

Raises:
    HTTPException: 401 if token invalid/expired or password incorrect

get.description:
Initiate OIDC authentication flow (public).

Redirects user to OIDC provider for authentication.

Returns:
    Redirect to OIDC provider authorization endpoint

post.description:
Test OIDC provider connection (admin only).

Tests connectivity and metadata retrieval without performing full auth flow.

Args:
    oidc_config: OIDC configuration to test

Returns:
    Test results with success/failure and any errors

Create initial admin account (first-time setup).

Only works if no admin account exists yet.
Automatically enables auth_mode='local' after setup.

get.description:
Get authentication status (public endpoint).

Returns setup completion status, auth mode, and OIDC enablement.

post.description:
Create a new backup of all settings.

Returns:
    Metadata about the created backup file

get.description:

This endpoint creates a backup on-the-fly and returns it directly.
Kept for backwards compatibility with old frontend code.

Download a specific backup file.

schema.title: Response Restore Backup Legacy Api V1 Backup Restore Post

This creates a safety backup before restoring.

Returns:

schema.title: Response Restore Backup Api V1 Backup Restore  Filename  Post

schema.title: Response Delete Backup Api V1 Backup  Filename  Delete

post.description:
Full cleanup: containers + images based on mode.

Uses current settings unless overridden by query parameters.

Modes:
- dangling: Only remove untagged images
- moderate: Remove untagged images + exited containers
- aggressive: Remove untagged images + exited containers + old unused images

schema.title: Response Cleanup All Api V1 Cleanup All Post

post.description:
Clean up exited/dead containers.

Removes containers that are in exited, dead, or created state.
Respects exclude patterns from settings.

schema.title: Response Cleanup Containers Api V1 Cleanup Containers Post

post.description:
Clean up Docker images.

Args:
    dangling_only: If True, only removes untagged images
    older_than_days: If provided, also removes images older than this threshold

parameters.description: Remove images older than X days
parameters.name: older_than_days

schema.description: Remove images older than X days
schema.title: Older Than Days
Response Cleanup Images Api V1 Cleanup Images Post

get.description:
Preview what would be cleaned up without removing anything.

Uses current settings unless overridden by query parameters.

schema.title: Response Preview Cleanup Api V1 Cleanup Preview Get

post.description:
Trigger immediate cleanup using current settings.

This runs the same cleanup that would run on schedule.
Useful for testing settings or manual cleanup.

schema.title: Response Run Cleanup Now Api V1 Cleanup Run Now Post

get.description:
Get current cleanup settings.

Returns all cleanup-related settings for display/editing.

schema.title: Response Get Cleanup Settings Api V1 Cleanup Settings Get

get.description:
Get Docker disk usage statistics.

Returns disk usage for images, containers, volumes, and build cache.

schema.title: Response Get Disk Usage Api V1 Cleanup Stats Get

get.description: Get a specific setting by key.

Comprehensive health check for monitoring.

Checks:
- Database connectivity
- Docker daemon availability
- Disk space

Returns:
    Health status with component details

Note: This endpoint is public (no authentication required) for health monitoring
Get system information.

Lightweight system metrics in Prometheus text format.

Returns a small subset of metrics (container counts, update counts by status)
using hand-built Prometheus text format. No external library required.

For the full Prometheus metrics endpoint (with detailed histograms, gauges,
and counters from the prometheus_client library), use GET /metrics instead.

Note: This endpoint is public (no authentication required) for Prometheus scraping.

Kubernetes readiness probe.

Simple endpoint that returns ready=true when the application is running.
This is useful for Kubernetes readiness probes.

Note: This endpoint is public (no authentication required) for K8s probes
Get version information.

List updates by status with pagination.

Args:
    status: Filter by status (pending, approved, rejected, applied). If None, returns all updates.
    container_id: Filter by container ID. If None, returns updates for all containers.
    skip: Number of records to skip (default: 0)
    limit: Maximum number of records to return (default: 100, max: 1000)

Returns:
    List of updates (excludes snoozed updates)

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return
schema.title: Limit
Response List Updates Api V1 Updates  Get

    Confirmation message

get.description: Get all pending updates.

schema.title: Response Get Pending Updates Api V1 Updates Pending Get

post.description:
Reload scheduler configuration from settings.

This allows updating the schedule without restarting the container.

Returns:
    Success message with new schedule

schema.title: Response Reload Scheduler Api V1 Updates Scheduler Reload Post

get.description:
Get background scheduler status.

Returns:
    Scheduler status and next run time

schema.title: Response Get Scheduler Status Api V1 Updates Scheduler Status Get

post.description:
Manually trigger an update check outside the schedule.

Returns:
    Success message

schema.title: Response Trigger Scheduler Api V1 Updates Scheduler Trigger Post

get.description: Get security-related updates.

schema.title: Response Get Security Updates Api V1 Updates Security Get

delete.description:
Delete an update record.

Args:
    update_id: Update ID

Returns:
    Success message

schema.title: Response Delete Update Api V1 Updates  Update Id  Delete

get.description:
Get update details.

Args:
    update_id: Update ID

Returns:
    Update details

post.description:
Apply an approved update.

This will:
1. Update the compose file
2. Execute docker compose up -d
3. Create history record
4. Update container status
5. Validate health check (if configured)

Args:
    update_id: Update ID
    request: Update apply request (includes triggered_by)

Returns:
    Result of the update operation

schema.title: Response Apply Update Api V1 Updates  Update Id  Apply Post

post.description:
Approve an update.

Args:
    update_id: Update ID
    approval: Approval details

Returns:
    Success message

schema.title: Response Approve Update Api V1 Updates  Update Id  Approve Post

post.description:
Cancel a pending retry and reset update to pending status.

Args:
    update_id: Update ID

Returns:
    Updated update object

Reject an update.

Args:
    update_id: Update ID
    reason: Optional rejection reason

Returns:
    Success message

schema.title: Response Reject Update Api V1 Updates  Update Id  Reject Post

post.description:
Remove a stale container from the database entirely.

This deletes both the update notification and the container record.
Use this for containers that are truly obsolete and will never return.

Args:
    update_id: Update ID (must be a stale-type update)

Returns:
    Success message

schema.title: Response Remove Container From Db Api V1 Updates  Update Id  Remove Container Post

post.description:
Snooze/dismiss a stale container notification.

Sets snoozed_until to current time + threshold days, preventing the
notification from showing up again until the snooze expires.

Args:
    update_id: Update ID

Returns:
    Success message with snooze details

schema.title: Response Snooze Update Api V1 Updates  Update Id  Snooze Post

post.description:
Create a new webhook.

SSRF Protection: Webhook URLs pointing to private/internal IPs are blocked.

Args:
    webhook: Webhook configuration
    admin: Authenticated admin user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Created webhook

Raises:
    400: Invalid webhook configuration or SSRF attempt
    409: Webhook with same name already exists

delete.description:
Delete a webhook.

Args:
    webhook_id: Webhook ID
    admin: Authenticated admin user
    db: Database session

Raises:
    404: Webhook not found

get.description:
Get webhook by ID.

Args:
    webhook_id: Webhook ID
    admin: Authenticated admin user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Webhook details

Raises:
    404: Webhook not found

put.description:
Update an existing webhook.

Args:
    webhook_id: Webhook ID
    webhook: Updated webhook data
    admin: Authenticated admin user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Updated webhook

Raises:
    400: Invalid webhook configuration or SSRF attempt
    404: Webhook not found

post.description:
Send a test payload to a webhook.

Args:
    webhook_id: Webhook ID
    admin: Authenticated admin user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Test result with success status and details

Raises:
    404: Webhook not found

get.description:
Get Homepage widget HTML.

Returns an HTML widget showing:
- Total containers
- Updates applied (successful)
- Updates skipped (no updates available)
- Updates failed
Get widget data as JSON for custom integrations.

schema.title: Response Get Widget Data Api V1 Widget Data Get

get.description: Health check endpoint.

Full Prometheus metrics endpoint using the prometheus_client library.

This is the primary scrape target for Prometheus. Includes detailed histograms,
gauges, and counters. For a lightweight subset (container/update counts only),
use GET /api/v1/system/metrics.
